Original Article Title: Copy Fail: What You Need to Know About the Most Severe Linux Threat in Years
Summary: The Linux kernel authencesn algorithm contains a memory corruption vulnerability where it improperly uses the destination buffer as a scratch pad. This flaw allows for an out-of-bounds write of four bytes, which has been addressed by reverting the affected optimization.

Original Article Title: Copy Fail: What You Need to Know About the Most Severe Linux Threat in Years
Summary: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) is a Linux kernel vulnerability in the authencesn cryptographic template that allows unprivileged local users to perform a controlled 4-byte write to the page cache. This flaw can be exploited to modify setuid binaries, enabling privilege escalation to root and container escape.

Original Article Title: Vibe Hacking: Two AI-Augmented Campaigns Target Government and Financial Sectors in Latin America

These campaigns target government and financial sectors in Latin America, employing advanced lateral movement and data exfiltration techniques via HTTP-based command-and-control infrastructure.

Original Article Title: Inside agenteV2: How Brazilian Attackers Use Fake Court Summons to Steal Banking Credentials in Real Time
Summary: The agenteV2 malware is a persistent WebSocket-based backdoor delivered via phishing emails that impersonate judicial summons. It utilizes a Pastebin-based dead-drop resolver to dynamically update its command-and-control (C2) infrastructure, allowing attackers to rotate IPs without redeploying the malware.
